verificationNote: Evidence items are constructed guidance for cloud security audits based on CSA CCM v4, NIST CSF 2.0, and NACSA Act 854 obligations. Not endorsed by any standards body -- verify against authoritative sources before use in formal assessments.
description: Evidence items for cloud security audit -- mapped to CCM control domains, requirements, and applicable regulatory obligations. For auditors conducting cloud security assessments and for security teams preparing for audit.

items.name: MFA enforcement configuration and compliance report
items.description: Evidence that MFA is enforced for all human users accessing cloud management consoles and APIs.
items.howToVerify: Export IAM user list and verify MFA device status for each user. Check conditional access policies for MFA enforcement. Attempt console login without MFA to confirm block.
whatGoodLooksLike: 100% MFA enrollment for all human IAM identities
Conditional access or SCP enforcing MFA for all actions
Hardware security keys on root/owner accounts
Phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2) for administrator accounts
commonGaps: MFA not enforced via policy -- relies on user voluntary enrollment
Service accounts with console access but no MFA
Root account MFA enabled but using virtual MFA instead of hardware key
Break-glass accounts without MFA

items.name: Least privilege access review records
items.description: Evidence of periodic access reviews demonstrating least privilege enforcement across cloud identities.
items.howToVerify: Review access review records for last 12 months. Sample 10 IAM policies and check for wildcard (*) actions. Verify IAM Access Analyzer or Recommender findings are actioned.
whatGoodLooksLike: Quarterly access reviews completed with documented actions
No wildcard (*) actions in production IAM policies
Unused permissions identified and removed via Access Analyzer
Custom roles used where built-in roles are too broad
commonGaps: Broad built-in roles (Administrator, Owner) assigned to multiple users
Access reviews conducted but remediation not completed
No tooling for unused permission detection
Wildcard actions in policies with no review

items.name: Service account and non-human identity inventory
items.description: Evidence that all service accounts, managed identities, and API keys are inventoried, reviewed, and managed with appropriate lifecycle controls.
items.howToVerify: Export service account list across all accounts/projects. Verify each has an owner. Check for long-lived access keys and their last usage date. Confirm workload identity federation where applicable.
whatGoodLooksLike: Complete service account inventory with assigned owners
No access keys older than 90 days without documented exception
Workload identity federation replacing static credentials
Unused service accounts disabled or deleted
commonGaps: Orphaned service accounts with no owner
Access keys older than 1 year still active
Static credentials in CI/CD pipelines instead of OIDC federation
Service accounts with admin-level permissions

items.name: Encryption at rest configuration evidence
items.description: Evidence that all data at rest is encrypted across storage, databases, and compute volumes.
items.howToVerify: Run CSPM scan for unencrypted resources. Check S3/Storage default encryption settings. Verify EBS/Disk encryption defaults. Confirm KMS CMK usage for sensitive data.
whatGoodLooksLike: Zero unencrypted storage buckets, database instances, or volumes
Customer-managed keys (CMK) used for sensitive and regulated data
Account-level default encryption enabled
CSPM rule continuously monitoring for unencrypted resources
commonGaps: Provider-managed encryption only -- no CMK for sensitive data
Legacy resources created before encryption defaults were enabled
Temporary or test resources without encryption
No automated detection of unencrypted resource creation

items.name: TLS configuration and minimum version enforcement
items.description: Evidence that TLS 1.2+ is enforced on all services and legacy versions are disabled.
items.howToVerify: Check TLS minimum version settings on load balancers, storage accounts, databases, and API endpoints. Scan externally with SSL Labs or similar. Verify S3 bucket policies deny non-HTTPS.
whatGoodLooksLike: TLS 1.2 minimum on all services (TLS 1.3 preferred where supported)
No TLS 1.0 or 1.1 endpoints
Weak cipher suites disabled
HTTPS-only enforced on storage and API endpoints
commonGaps: Legacy applications still on TLS 1.0/1.1
Storage endpoints accessible via HTTP without redirect
Internal services using unencrypted protocols
Certificate expiry monitoring not configured

items.name: Data classification register and DLP policy evidence
items.description: Evidence that data is classified, labelled, and protected by DLP policies appropriate to classification level.
items.howToVerify: Review data classification policy. Check automated discovery scan results (Macie, Purview, Cloud DLP). Verify DLP policies are active and generating findings. Sample test DLP blocking on classified data.
whatGoodLooksLike: Data classification policy with defined levels (public, internal, confidential, restricted)
Automated data discovery scanning all major data stores
DLP policies blocking exfiltration of classified data
Resource tags reflecting classification level
commonGaps: Classification policy exists but not enforced technically
No automated data discovery -- manual classification only
DLP deployed but in monitor-only mode with no blocking
Classification tags missing from resources

items.name: Network architecture diagram with segmentation evidence
items.description: Evidence that cloud networks are designed with proper segmentation, subnet isolation, and defence-in-depth architecture.
items.howToVerify: Review network architecture diagram. Verify VPC/VNet separation between environments. Check subnet design (public/private/data tiers). Confirm hub-and-spoke or transit gateway topology.
whatGoodLooksLike: Current network diagram dated within 6 months
Separate VPCs/VNets for production, staging, and development
Public, private, and data subnets with appropriate route tables
Centralised ingress/egress through hub network
commonGaps: Flat network with all workloads in single VPC/VNet
No separation between production and non-production
Diagram outdated or non-existent
Direct internet access from database subnets

items.name: Security group and firewall rule audit results
items.description: Evidence that security groups and firewall rules follow least-privilege deny-by-default configuration.
items.howToVerify: Export all security groups/NSGs/firewall rules. Check for 0.0.0.0/0 ingress on management ports. Verify rules have business justification documentation. Confirm quarterly review records.
whatGoodLooksLike: No 0.0.0.0/0 ingress on SSH (22), RDP (3389), or database ports
All rules documented with business justification and expiry date
Quarterly rule review records with stale rule removal
Automated detection of overly permissive rule creation
commonGaps: 0.0.0.0/0 ingress on management ports (SSH, RDP)
Security groups with hundreds of rules and no documentation
No review process -- rules accumulate over time
Allow-all outbound with no egress filtering

items.name: Private endpoint deployment and public access restriction evidence
items.description: Evidence that PaaS services are accessed via private endpoints and public network access is disabled.
items.howToVerify: List all PaaS resources (databases, storage, key vaults). Verify private endpoints exist. Check public network access is disabled. Confirm DNS resolution uses private zones.
whatGoodLooksLike: Private endpoints deployed for all databases, storage, and key management services
Public network access disabled on PaaS resources
Private DNS zones configured for name resolution
VPC endpoint policies restricting accessible resources
commonGaps: PaaS services accessible from public internet
Private endpoints deployed but public access not disabled
DNS resolution still using public endpoints
No VPC endpoint policies restricting service access

items.name: Cloud audit logging configuration and coverage report
items.description: Evidence that comprehensive audit logging is enabled across all cloud accounts and regions.
items.howToVerify: Verify CloudTrail/Activity Log/Audit Logs enabled in all accounts and regions. Check data event logging for sensitive resources. Confirm logs stored in protected centralised location.
whatGoodLooksLike: Organisation-level audit trail covering all accounts and regions
Data event logging enabled for sensitive storage and databases
Logs stored in dedicated log archive account with immutable storage
Log integrity validation enabled
commonGaps: Audit logging not enabled in all regions
Data event logging disabled -- only management events captured
Logs stored in same account as production workloads
No log integrity validation

items.name: SIEM integration evidence and detection rule coverage
items.description: Evidence that cloud logs are integrated with SIEM and cloud-specific detection rules are deployed.
items.howToVerify: Verify log sources flowing to SIEM. Check cloud-specific detection rules are active. Review sample alert investigations from last 30 days. Confirm alert routing to on-call personnel.
whatGoodLooksLike: All cloud log sources integrated with SIEM
Cloud-specific detection rules tuned and active
Alert investigation records for last 30 days
Severity-based alert routing with defined SLAs
commonGaps: Only audit logs in SIEM -- missing flow logs, DNS, WAF, threat detection
Generic detection rules without cloud-specific tuning
Alerts generated but not reviewed or investigated
No defined alert response SLAs

items.name: Log retention configuration and compliance evidence
items.description: Evidence that log retention periods meet regulatory and organisational requirements.
items.howToVerify: Check retention settings on log storage. Verify immutable storage configuration. Confirm retention meets regulatory requirements (NACSA s26 if applicable). Review capacity monitoring.
whatGoodLooksLike: Minimum 12 months online retention for audit logs
Immutable storage (WORM) for compliance-required logs
Tiered retention with lifecycle management
Capacity monitoring with alerting before exhaustion
commonGaps: Default retention only (90 days or less)
Logs in modifiable storage without integrity controls
No capacity monitoring -- logs silently deleted on overflow
Retention not aligned to regulatory requirements

items.name: Cloud incident response plan and tabletop exercise records
items.description: Evidence that a cloud-specific IR plan exists and has been tested through tabletop exercises.
items.howToVerify: Review cloud IR plan for completeness. Check last tabletop exercise date and results. Verify CSP engagement procedures are documented. Confirm contact lists are current.
whatGoodLooksLike: Cloud-specific IR plan covering common attack scenarios
Tabletop exercise conducted within last 12 months
CSP support engagement procedure documented
Contact lists current and tested
commonGaps: IT IR plan used without cloud-specific procedures
No tabletop exercises conducted
CSP engagement not documented -- team unsure how to engage cloud vendor during incident
Contact lists outdated

items.name: Forensic readiness configuration and evidence collection procedures
items.description: Evidence that cloud environments are pre-configured for forensic investigation.
items.howToVerify: Verify comprehensive logging enabled. Check forensic snapshot procedures. Confirm dedicated forensic environment exists. Review evidence collection automation.
whatGoodLooksLike: All audit and data access logs enabled
Automated disk snapshot procedure documented and tested
Dedicated forensic account/project with isolated environment
Chain of custody procedures for cloud evidence
commonGaps: Insufficient logging for forensic investigation
No procedure for capturing disk snapshots during incident
Forensic analysis performed in production environment
No chain of custody for cloud evidence

items.name: Containment automation and runbook evidence
items.description: Evidence that automated containment procedures exist for common cloud incident scenarios.
items.howToVerify: Review containment runbooks. Test containment automation in non-production. Verify rollback procedures exist. Check containment SLA targets.
whatGoodLooksLike: Automated containment for credential compromise, instance compromise, and data exposure
Containment tested in non-production environment
Rollback procedures for each containment action
Sub-15-minute containment SLA for critical incidents
commonGaps: Containment relies entirely on manual steps
Containment procedures not tested
No rollback procedures -- containment may cause outage
No defined containment SLA

items.name: Container image scanning results and remediation records
items.description: Evidence that container images are scanned before deployment and critical vulnerabilities are remediated.
items.howToVerify: Review image scanning pipeline configuration. Check scan results for production images. Verify critical/high vulnerability remediation SLA. Confirm deployment blocking for non-compliant images.
whatGoodLooksLike: All images scanned in CI/CD before deployment
Zero critical vulnerabilities in production images
Vulnerability remediation SLA tracked and met
Deployment blocked for images failing scan
commonGaps: Images deployed without scanning
Scanning enabled but results not reviewed
Critical vulnerabilities in production with no remediation timeline
Base images not regularly updated

items.name: Kubernetes CIS benchmark scan results
items.description: Evidence that container orchestrator configurations comply with CIS Kubernetes Benchmark.
items.howToVerify: Run kube-bench or equivalent CIS scan. Review findings and remediation status. Check pod security standards enforcement. Verify RBAC configuration.
whatGoodLooksLike: CIS Kubernetes Benchmark scan within last quarter
Level 1 controls fully implemented
Pod Security Standards (restricted profile) enforced in production
RBAC with least-privilege namespace-level roles
commonGaps: No CIS benchmark scanning
Default pod security settings (no restrictions)
Cluster-admin bound to service accounts unnecessarily
API server accessible from public network

items.name: Serverless function IAM role audit and configuration evidence
items.description: Evidence that serverless functions use least-privilege IAM roles and sensitive configuration is properly managed.
items.howToVerify: Export all function IAM roles and permissions. Check for overly permissive policies. Verify secrets stored in secret manager (not environment variables). Review VPC configuration for private resource access.
whatGoodLooksLike: Each function has a dedicated IAM role with minimal permissions
No wildcard (*) resource or action permissions
Secrets stored in secret manager with encrypted references
Functions placed in VPC when accessing private resources
commonGaps: Single shared IAM role for all functions
Admin-level permissions on function roles
Secrets hardcoded in environment variables or source code
No VPC placement for functions accessing databases

items.name: API gateway authentication and rate limiting configuration
items.description: Evidence that API gateways are configured with authentication, rate limiting, and input validation.
items.howToVerify: Review API gateway configuration for authentication requirements. Verify rate limiting and throttling policies. Check request validation rules. Confirm WAF integration.
whatGoodLooksLike: Authentication required on all API endpoints (OAuth 2.0/OIDC)
Rate limiting configured per client
Request schema validation enabled
WAF deployed on API gateway
commonGaps: API endpoints without authentication
No rate limiting -- vulnerable to abuse
No request validation -- accepting arbitrary input
No WAF protection on API endpoints

items.name: CI/CD pipeline security configuration and credential management evidence
items.description: Evidence that CI/CD pipelines are hardened with proper secret management and authentication.
items.howToVerify: Review pipeline configuration for secret management. Check authentication method (OIDC vs static credentials). Verify branch protection rules. Confirm build artifact signing.
whatGoodLooksLike: OIDC federation for cloud authentication (no static credentials)
Secrets stored in secret manager (not pipeline variables or code)
Branch protection with required reviews on main branch
Build artifacts signed with provenance
commonGaps: Long-lived access keys stored in pipeline secrets
Secrets in pipeline variables without encryption
No branch protection -- direct push to main allowed
No artifact signing or provenance

items.name: IaC scanning results and SBOM generation evidence
items.description: Evidence that infrastructure-as-code is scanned for misconfigurations and SBOMs are generated for deployments.
items.howToVerify: Review IaC scanning pipeline integration. Check scan results and remediation records. Verify SBOM generation for production builds. Confirm SBOM stored in central repository.
whatGoodLooksLike: IaC scanning integrated in CI/CD with blocking on critical findings
Custom scanning rules for organisation-specific policies
SBOM generated for all production builds (SPDX or CycloneDX)
SBOM repository with lifecycle management
commonGaps: No IaC scanning -- misconfigurations reach production
Scanning enabled but findings not reviewed or remediated
No SBOM generation
No process for CVE-to-SBOM correlation when new vulnerabilities disclosed

items.name: KMS key inventory and access policy evidence
items.description: Evidence that all encryption operations use cloud KMS and key access policies enforce least privilege.
items.howToVerify: Export KMS key inventory. Verify key policies restrict access to authorised principals. Check for any application-managed keys outside KMS. Confirm audit logging enabled for all key operations.
whatGoodLooksLike: Complete KMS key inventory with purpose and owner for each key
Key policies enforce least-privilege access
No encryption keys stored in application code or config files
All KMS API calls logged in audit trail
commonGaps: Encryption keys stored in application configuration files
KMS key policies with broad access (full account access)
No key usage audit logging
Orphaned keys with no documented purpose or owner

items.name: Key rotation configuration and compliance evidence
items.description: Evidence that automatic key rotation is enabled and rotation schedules are appropriate to data sensitivity.
items.howToVerify: Check automatic rotation settings on all KMS keys. Verify rotation periods align to data classification. Confirm old key versions are disabled after grace period. Review rotation audit logs.
whatGoodLooksLike: Automatic rotation enabled on all KMS keys
Rotation period aligned to data sensitivity (90 days sensitive, 365 days standard)
Old key versions disabled after grace period
Rotation compliance monitored via CSPM
commonGaps: Automatic rotation not enabled
Keys older than 2 years with no rotation
No monitoring of rotation compliance
Old key versions never disabled

items.name: CSPM scan results and remediation records
items.description: Evidence that CSPM tooling is deployed and findings are tracked and remediated within defined SLAs.
items.howToVerify: Review CSPM dashboard for current compliance score. Check finding remediation records against SLA (critical: 24h, high: 7d, medium: 30d). Verify coverage across all accounts.
whatGoodLooksLike: CSPM enabled across all cloud accounts and regions
Compliance score tracked over time with improving trend
Critical and high findings remediated within SLA
Automated remediation for common findings
commonGaps: CSPM not deployed or only in primary account
Findings generated but not tracked or remediated
No defined remediation SLAs
Alert fatigue from untuned CSPM rules

items.name: CIS benchmark compliance report and exception register
items.description: Evidence of CIS benchmark compliance assessment and documented exceptions.
items.howToVerify: Review most recent CIS benchmark scan results. Check compliance percentage by section. Review exception register for non-compliant items with risk acceptance.
whatGoodLooksLike: CIS Level 1 fully compliant in all production accounts
CIS Level 2 applied to production workloads
Exception register with risk acceptance for non-compliant items
Continuous monitoring with automated alerting on new non-compliance
commonGaps: No CIS benchmark assessment conducted
Assessment conducted once but never repeated
Non-compliance with no exception register or risk acceptance
CIS assessment in primary account only -- not all accounts

items.name: Resource tagging compliance report
items.description: Evidence that mandatory resource tags are enforced across all cloud resources.
items.howToVerify: Run tagging compliance report across all accounts. Check enforcement policies (deny creation without tags). Verify mandatory tag set includes owner, environment, data-classification, cost-centre.
whatGoodLooksLike: Greater than 95% tagging compliance across all resources
Policy enforcement denying untagged resource creation
Mandatory tags: owner, environment, data-classification, cost-centre
Tagging compliance tracked and reported monthly
commonGaps: No mandatory tags defined or enforced
Tags defined but no enforcement mechanism
Low compliance percentage with no improvement plan
Tags applied inconsistently with no standard taxonomy

items.name: Backup configuration and coverage audit
items.description: Evidence that all cloud workloads are covered by appropriate backup policies.
items.howToVerify: Review backup configuration for all databases, compute, and storage. Verify backup frequency aligns to RPO. Check backup encryption and immutability settings. Confirm backup coverage against resource inventory.
whatGoodLooksLike: All production databases and compute backed up per policy
Backup frequency aligned to RPO requirements
Backups encrypted with customer-managed keys
Immutable backups (vault lock) for ransomware protection
commonGaps: Backup not configured for all production resources
Backup frequency not aligned to RPO -- potential data loss exceeds tolerance
Backups in same account as production -- vulnerable to account compromise
No immutable backups -- vulnerable to ransomware

items.name: DR test results and RTO/RPO validation
items.description: Evidence that DR testing has been conducted and RTO/RPO targets have been validated.
items.howToVerify: Review last DR test report. Compare actual recovery time to RTO target. Verify RPO was met (no data loss beyond tolerance). Check remediation actions from previous tests.
whatGoodLooksLike: DR test conducted within last 12 months
Actual recovery time within defined RTO
Data loss within defined RPO
Remediation actions from previous tests completed
commonGaps: No DR testing conducted
DR test limited to backup restore -- no full failover test
RTO/RPO targets not defined or not validated
Test findings not remediated

items.name: Cloud governance framework and landing zone evidence
items.description: Evidence that a cloud governance framework is established with guardrails, policies, and accountability.
items.howToVerify: Review cloud governance policy document. Check account/subscription structure. Verify SCPs/policies enforcing guardrails. Confirm landing zone deployment.
whatGoodLooksLike: Board-approved cloud security policy
Landing zone deployed with security guardrails
Account vending process with security baseline
Approved service catalogue and architecture patterns
commonGaps: No formal cloud governance framework
Accounts created ad hoc without security baseline
No guardrails -- relying on user awareness for security
Governance framework exists but not enforced

items.name: Policy-as-code deployment and compliance dashboard
items.description: Evidence that security policies are implemented as code with automated enforcement.
items.howToVerify: Review policy-as-code repository. Verify policies deployed across all accounts. Check compliance dashboard for policy adherence. Confirm exception management process.
whatGoodLooksLike: Security policies managed as code in version control
Policies deployed via CI/CD to all accounts
Compliance dashboard showing policy adherence percentage
Time-limited exceptions with documented risk acceptance
commonGaps: Policies documented but not implemented as code
Policy-as-code deployed in primary account only
No compliance dashboard or monitoring
Permanent exceptions without review

items.name: CSP compliance reports and vendor assessment records
items.description: Evidence that CSP compliance reports are collected and vendor security assessments conducted.
items.howToVerify: Request current CSP SOC 2 and ISO 27001 reports. Review shared responsibility model documentation. Check SaaS vendor assessment records. Verify CAIQ responses reviewed.
whatGoodLooksLike: Current CSP SOC 2 Type II report reviewed within last 12 months
Shared responsibility model documented with obligations mapped
SaaS vendor assessments completed for critical vendors
CAIQ or equivalent questionnaire responses reviewed
commonGaps: CSP compliance reports never requested or reviewed
Shared responsibility model not understood -- security gaps at boundary
No SaaS vendor security assessment process
Relying solely on CSP certifications without reviewing report content
